introSection: Hyperion is a steel hyper roller coaster located at Energylandia in Zator, Małopolskie, Poland. It was manufactured by Intamin and took the title of tallest and fastest hyper coaster in Europe from Shambhala at PortAventura Park at tall with a drop at  tall. The ride opened on 14 July 2018. It has the tallest drop of any hyper roller coaster, and the tallest roller coaster of all to have an inversion.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
In October 2016, Energylandia announced that it would be building a hyper roller coaster, planned to open in 2018. The park revealed two proposals, which it claimed were from Vekoma and Intamin, and asked fans to choose their favourite. The following month, Energylandia announced that Intamin had been selected to design and build the roller coaster.

The name of the coaster was revealed to be Hyperion on 15 October 2017 through the park's Facebook page. Later that month, concept art was released revealing Hyperion's sci-fi theme.

The highest point of the ride was topped off in April 2018. The track layout was completed by the end of May. The first test run was completed on 28 June 2018.

The ride opened on 14 July 2018.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: This roller coaster has an 85° drop which dives into a tunnel.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: On 16 August 2018, a staff member was struck and killed by a train while retrieving a lost mobile phone. The roller coaster was reopened the following day.

fullText:
Hyperion is a steel hyper roller coaster located at Energylandia in Zator, Małopolskie, Poland. It was manufactured by Intamin and took the title of tallest and fastest hyper coaster in Europe from Shambhala at PortAventura Park at tall with a drop at  tall. The ride opened on 14 July 2018. It has the tallest drop of any hyper roller coaster, and the tallest roller coaster of all to have an inversion.

In October 2016, Energylandia announced that it would be building a hyper roller coaster, planned to open in 2018. The park revealed two proposals, which it claimed were from Vekoma and Intamin, and asked fans to choose their favourite. The following month, Energylandia announced that Intamin had been selected to design and build the roller coaster.

The name of the coaster was revealed to be Hyperion on 15 October 2017 through the park's Facebook page. Later that month, concept art was released revealing Hyperion's sci-fi theme.

The highest point of the ride was topped off in April 2018. The track layout was completed by the end of May. The first test run was completed on 28 June 2018.

The ride opened on 14 July 2018.

This roller coaster has an 85° drop which dives into a tunnel.



On 16 August 2018, a staff member was struck and killed by a train while retrieving a lost mobile phone. The roller coaster was reopened the following day.
